rationale: Postcondition reduces to: r1.v@ == empty && r2.v@ == empty ==> r1 == r2, where the struct's single field is v: Vec<K>. Verus's Vec view is uninterpreted (vstd/view.rs:107) and there is no Vec extensional-equality axiom: vstd provides axiom_slice_ext_equal and axiom_array_ext_equal, but no axiom_vec_ext_equal. Tried: extensional eq via =~= on the inner Vec, broadcast use of group_vec_axioms / group_seq_axioms / group_seq_lib_default / group_set_axioms, explicit length facts (axiom_spec_len), and constructing the implication via assert(...) by { if H { ... } }. Z3 cannot derive r1.v == r2.v from r1.v@ == r2.v@ for Vec<K> without such an axiom, and the constraints forbid assume/admit/external_body or introducing new fn/lemma items.
